executive_summary: In highlight coverage, our automatic token support for Robinhood Chain launched via Crowdfund Insider, while our stablecoin infrastructure research projecting $1.5 quadrillion annual processing by 2035 was featured in CoinMarketCap and our address clustering ontology standardization work appeared in KuCoin's compliance guide. In crypto news, Swift unveiled a blockchain system for 24/7 cross-border payments (Bloomberg), Paradigm raised $1.2 billion for AI-focused crypto bets (Bloomberg), and India's central bank intensified its push to isolate banks from crypto amid evasion concerns (Reuters)—signalling rising regulatory friction in emerging markets. In competitor news: Arkham capitalized on SpaceX Bitcoin wallet speculation across Yahoo Finance and crypto outlets; Nansen's Trump memecoin analysis showing $3.8 billion in losses broke through The New York Times and CoinDesk; Elliptic partnered with CoinGecko on tokenized asset pricing (Crypto Briefing); TRM Labs released North Korea theft data claiming two-thirds of H1 2026 crypto hacks (upi.com, The Korea Herald); and Merkle Science was mentioned in crypto recovery roundups.

market_landscape.title: Regulatory Friction Accelerates
market_landscape.body: India's central bank doubled down on banking isolation while tax authorities flagged evasion risks, signalling emerging markets tightening restrictions faster than developed ones. US sanctions enforcement on ISIS-K addresses demonstrates real-time Treasury-exchange coordination, raising compliance bar for all platforms.

market_landscape.title: Infrastructure Layer Consolidation
market_landscape.body: Swift's blockchain payments system and stablecoin growth projections ($1.5T by 2035) indicate institutional payment infrastructure becoming crypto-native. Competitor focus on tokenized asset pricing (Elliptic-CoinGecko) shows market prioritizing settlement and pricing data over pure compliance.

market_landscape.title: Geopolitical Volatility Driving Volatility
market_landscape.body: Trump's Iran deal reversal, Russia-Ukraine escalation, and BRICS dollar-alternative rhetoric triggered multi-asset volatility (FTSE worst day since May, Indian benchmarks biggest loss in three months). Crypto's status as geopolitical hedge and sanctions-evasion vector makes our enforcement and intelligence positioning more critical.
